prompt: A hyper-realistic, award-winning 35mm photograph of a medieval blacksmith's workshop shrouded in pitch-black, deep shadows. In the center spotlight, a rigid, highly polished metal suit of armor has been meticulously hammered to perfectly encase a heap of completely random, un-armor-like debris: a massive jagged stag antler, a smashed wooden wagon wheel hub, and a rough boulder. To guarantee perfect physical separation without blending, thick, dark velvet cushioning completely wraps the debris; there is a crisp, sharp, visible separation line between the metal armor and the wood and bone beneath it. Right next to this tangled pile sits a single, smooth, perfectly round river stone—a 'new' object the hyper-specific armor clearly cannot fit. The armor is frozen in a grotesquely contorted shape. In the foreground, on a separate clean wooden table, is a pristine iron sword, entirely out of reach. Beside the armor stands a frustrated blacksmith holding metallic measuring calipers. A blinding, god-ray shaft of pure sunlight directly illuminates a clean, standard, highly functional steel breastplate hanging on the back wall, making it the absolute brightest, most eye-catching focal point against the dark shadows. Volumetric lighting, masterpiece, photorealistic, ultra-crisp focus. Absolutely no text, no writing, no floating elements, no mutated hands, no blended textures, no melting objects, completely separate materials.
metaphor: Rigid armor meticulously molded to irregular junk (noisy training data) fails to fit a simple round stone (new test data), contrasted with a functional, generalized breastplate.
